journal_entry.description: Client entertainment meal paid with corporate credit card
journal_entry.rationale: Client entertainment meal is recorded as Meals and Entertainment Expense. Under IFRS, meals and entertainment expenses are generally only 50% deductible for tax purposes in Canada, but the full amount is recorded as an expense. The corporate credit card creates a liability (Credit Card Payable). No HST/GST is recoverable on meals and entertainment expenses in Canada per CRA rules, so no tax receivable line is added even though the restaurant would have charged tax (the $125 is treated as the all-in amount paid).

output.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase (credit card payable). The meal expense is on the debit side.

credit_classifier.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase (credit card payable). The meal expense is on the debit side.

output.reason: Client entertainment meal = expense increase. The $125 meal is a business entertainment expense incurred to entertain clients. This is debited to an expense account (e.g., Meals & Entertainment Expense).

debit_classifier.reason: Client entertainment meal = expense increase. The $125 meal is a business entertainment expense incurred to entertain clients. This is debited to an expense account (e.g., Meals & Entertainment Expense).
